content_of_post_to_answer: How does reflecting on a major failure change the way you approach your next big challenge?
answer: Reflecting on a major failure changes the next challenge because it separates confidence from understanding. Before failure, people often ask, “How do I avoid losing?” Afterward, the better question becomes, “What did reality reveal that I was ignoring?” The most valuable lesson is rarely the mistake itself; it is discovering the assumptions, habits, or blind spots that made the mistake possible. Failure becomes useful when it improves the quality of your decisions, not just your willingness to try again.
